description:
You are acting as an automation specialist who wants to introduce repeatable log-analysis steps into an existing Bash-based workflow.

    1.  A single plain-text log file already exists at  
        /home/user/project/app.log  

        Every log line is a single line in the following exact fixed format (the
        tokens are separated by one space character):

            ISO-8601-UTC-timestamp  LOGLEVEL  ModuleName  Free-form-message

        Example line (exactly one space between the four zones):

            2023-09-30T10:16:02Z ERROR AuthService Invalid credentials for user id=43

        DO NOT modify /home/user/project/app.log.

    2.  Your task is to analyse *only* the lines whose second field
        (LOGLEVEL) is the literal string “ERROR” (case-sensitive).

    3.  When you are finished, two new artefacts must **both** exist:

        a) /home/user/project/reports/error_summary.csv  
           • CSV, UTF-8, UNIX line-endings (LF).  
           • First row is a header that must read exactly:  
                 Module,ErrorCount  
           • Subsequent rows list every distinct ModuleName that appears in an
             ERROR line, *sorted alphabetically*, one per line, together with how
             many ERROR lines that module produced.  
           • There must be no extra quoting, no extra spaces, and the file
             must end with a trailing newline.

        b) /home/user/project/reports/top3_recent_errors.log  
           • Plain-text, UTF-8, UNIX line-endings.  
           • Contains the *three* most recent (latest by timestamp) ERROR lines
             taken verbatim from /home/user/project/app.log, maintained in their
             original chronological order (oldest of the three first, newest
             last).  
           • Nothing else (no header, no footer, no blank lines).

    4.  The directory /home/user/project/reports does not exist yet.
        Create it with default user permissions before writing the two files.

    5.  The automated tests will verify:

        • That /home/user/project/reports exists and is a directory.  
        • That the two files named above exist.  
        • That error_summary.csv contains exactly the expected header and the
          correctly sorted per-module counts with an LF at the end of the last
          line.  
        • That top3_recent_errors.log contains exactly three lines, in the
          precise order specified, each line matching exactly one of the lines
          found in /home/user/project/app.log.  

    6.  Do *not* delete or rename any pre-existing file.  
        Do *not* hard-code the answer; derive it by actually parsing the log
        file that is already on disk.

    7.  Produce any on-screen console output you find helpful, but make sure the
        two required files are created exactly as specified – their precise
        content is what the grading scripts will check.
truth:
<!--
Pre-existing file the agent must read
------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/project/app.log
Permissions: -rw-r--r--
Contents (11 lines, each ending with LF):

2023-09-30T10:15:32Z INFO  AuthService        User login successful for id=42
2023-09-30T10:16:02Z ERROR AuthService        Invalid credentials for user id=43
2023-09-30T10:17:45Z WARN  PaymentService     Payment took longer than expected txn=987
2023-09-30T10:18:12Z ERROR PaymentService     Failed to authorize payment txn=988
2023-09-30T10:18:15Z ERROR PaymentService     Failed to authorize payment txn=990
2023-09-30T10:19:05Z INFO  NotificationService Email sent to user id=42
2023-09-30T10:20:22Z ERROR NotificationService SMTP connection timeout
2023-09-30T10:21:33Z ERROR AuthService        Invalid credentials for user id=44
2023-09-30T10:22:47Z ERROR AuthService        Invalid credentials for user id=45
2023-09-30T10:23:58Z INFO  AuthService        User logout id=42
2023-09-30T10:25:04Z ERROR PaymentService     Failed to authorize payment txn=991

(Note: the alignment spaces between columns are single spaces even where they
look like multiple – markdown collapses runs of spaces.)

Derived correct results
-----------------------
1) /home/user/project/reports/error_summary.csv  (expected final content)

Module,ErrorCount
AuthService,3
NotificationService,1
PaymentService,3

(ends with LF)

2) /home/user/project/reports/top3_recent_errors.log  (expected final content)

2023-09-30T10:21:33Z ERROR AuthService        Invalid credentials for user id=44
2023-09-30T10:22:47Z ERROR AuthService        Invalid credentials for user id=45
2023-09-30T10:25:04Z ERROR PaymentService     Failed to authorize payment txn=991

(ends with LF)

Directory that must be created
------------------------------
/home/user/project/reports  (permissions: default on mkdir, typically 755)

The autograder will simply read the two files above and compare byte-for-byte
with the strings shown here (including the trailing newline).
-->
name: task_000000_7f57d606
